preamble: The palatalization of the velar stop [k] into the voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ] is a complex shift involving both palatalization, affrication, and voicing, often occurring before front vowels.

examples.note: While Italian primarily shows [tʃ], dialectal variation and historical stages involve voicing to [dʒ] in specific contexts.
Palatalization of Proto-Germanic *g, but related to the general palatalization of velars in the proximity of front vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
